project.name: TeleMed
project.description: Telemedicine platform for online doctor consultations, prescription management, and medical record storage.

database.notes: Provisioned via Fly Postgres dedicated cluster. Dedicated cluster used for HIPAA data isolation requirements.

auth.strategy: JWT (access 15min + refresh HttpOnly cookie 7d, TOTP for doctor/admin)
auth.notes: TOTP enforced at middleware level for doctor and admin roles. Refresh token stored server-side for revocation support.

integrations.name: Daily.co
integrations.purpose: Video consultation sessions for appointments. TeleMed does not store any video/audio data.

integrations.name: Resend
integrations.purpose: Transactional email: prescription ready notification, appointment reminders, password reset links.
integrations.notes: PHI may appear in email body only after BAA is active. Subject lines must not contain PHI.

integrations.name: AWS S3
integrations.purpose: Private storage for medical record file uploads (PHI). Files accessed via pre-signed URL only.

integrations.name: Twilio
integrations.purpose: SMS notifications for appointment reminders and prescription ready alerts.

infrastructure.redisNotes: BAA available on Upstash enterprise plan. Required for HIPAA compliance given task payloads may contain PHI references.
infrastructure.deploymentNotes: api and worker deployed as separate Fly Apps in the same Fly.io organization. Celery Beat runs as a separate Fly Machine within the worker app.

compliance.notes: All PHI access events trigger an audit_log write regardless of success/failure. Audit log is append-only; no UPDATE or DELETE permitted on audit_log rows.

observability.distributedTracingNote: OTel tracing deferred to v1.1 — api and worker share the same database and cross-service call volume in v1 is low (only Celery task dispatch). Full OTel instrumentation planned before v1.1 launch.

packages.name: telemed-types
packages.description: Shared TypeScript types and Python dataclasses for API contracts (Appointment, Prescription, MedicalRecord, User).

packages.name: telemed-config
packages.description: Shared ESLint, Prettier, and base TypeScript/Python configuration. Includes environment variable validation schemas.

models.name: User
models.notes: email stored as HMAC-SHA256 hash for lookups + encrypted value for display. role: patient | doctor | admin.

models.name: PatientProfile
models.notes: dob and ssn are PHI, stored encrypted. phone stored as hash + encrypted value.

models.name: Appointment
models.notes: reason is PHI, stored encrypted. status: scheduled | in_progress | completed | cancelled.

models.name: Prescription
models.notes: medication_name, dosage, instructions are PHI, all stored encrypted.

models.name: MedicalRecord
models.notes: title is PHI, stored encrypted. s3_key is the S3 object key for the private bucket; never exposed directly.

models.name: Diagnosis
models.notes: diagnosis_text is PHI, stored encrypted. icd_code is a standard code, not encrypted.

models.name: AuditLog
models.notes: Append-only table. No UPDATE or DELETE allowed at the application or DB layer. Retained for 7 years per HIPAA.

celeryTasks.name: send_appointment_reminder
celeryTasks.description: Sends email (Resend) and SMS (Twilio) reminder to the patient.

celeryTasks.name: send_prescription_ready_notification
celeryTasks.description: Sends email (Resend) to the patient notifying them a prescription is available.

celeryTasks.name: process_daily_webhook
celeryTasks.trigger: POST /webhooks/daily (dispatched to Celery after signature verification)
celeryTasks.description: Updates appointment status to completed and records session duration.

assumptions.statement: Fly.io deployment chosen: api and worker deployed as separate Fly Apps within the same Fly.io organization for resource isolation and independent scaling.
PostgreSQL 16 provisioned via Fly Postgres as a dedicated cluster. Dedicated (non-shared) cluster used for HIPAA data isolation — no co-tenancy with other projects.
Redis provisioned via Upstash Redis. BAA is available on the Upstash enterprise plan and must be signed before launch, as Celery task payloads may reference PHI entity IDs.

PHI fields (patientName, dateOfBirth, ssn, diagnosis, medicationName, instructions) are encrypted with AES-256-GCM before write to PostgreSQL. Encryption key stored in PHI_ENCRYPTION_KEY environment variable, not in codebase.
Searchable PHI fields (email, phone) are stored as HMAC-SHA256 hash alongside the AES-256-GCM encrypted value. Lookups use the hash; decrypted value is returned only to authorized roles.
Daily.co webhook payload signature verified via HMAC-SHA256 using DAILY_WEBHOOK_SECRET on every incoming request to POST /webhooks/daily. Unverified requests are rejected with HTTP 401.
Celery Beat scheduler runs as a separate Fly Machine within the worker Fly App. A single Beat instance is enforced to prevent duplicate scheduled task dispatch.
Medical record files uploaded to a private AWS S3 bucket with SSE-S3 server-side encryption. Pre-signed download URLs expire in 5 minutes (300 seconds). No public-read ACL permitted.
All PHI access events (read, write, download) are logged to the audit_log table with actor_id, action, entity_type, entity_id, ip_address, and timestamp. Audit log rows are append-only.
TOTP (2FA via django-otp) is enforced at Django middleware level for all accounts with the doctor or admin role. Login succeeds but subsequent API calls return HTTP 403 until TOTP is verified.

Sentry beforeSend hook configured to strip PHI fields (patientName, ssn, diagnosis) from all error events before transmission to Sentry servers. This is required because Sentry is not a HIPAA-covered entity under BAA in the free/team plan.
structlog configured with a redact processor for the following keys: password, token, ssn, diagnosis, medication_name. Redacted values are replaced with [REDACTED] in all log output.
